briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — aggressive summer approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for summer with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Pick a crankbait that goes deeper than the depth you're fishing, reel at a pace where you keep it in control. When it hits a rock, try to get it to crawl up and over and then back down the other side -- looks like a crawfish slowly scooting, acts like something between a normal crankbait 

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Palaniuk: 'Pick a crankbait that goes deeper than the depth you're fishing, reel at a pace where you keep it in control. When it hits a rock, try to get it to crawl up and over and then back down the
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Hackney: 'On a KVD 2.5, replace standard No.2 hooks with short shank No.1 around heavy cover for greater lip-grabbing ability.'
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Johnston: On the Potomac, I found fish out in the grass, not in the real thick stuff. I was casting to catch them, not flipping. When that failed due to muddy water from rain, I moved to flipping grass on the Greenway Flats.

KVD: Square bills are round-bodied with a steep angle and a square bill — this gives them a wide wobble and the best deflection qualities of any crankbait. Most weedless crankbait you can throw. Good through grass, brush, gnarly rocks, riprap. Wor...

Robertson: Spring crankbait progression -- 1) Prespawn: slow-roll lipless on flats near channel banks in 1-6 feet; 2) Postspawn: big-bodied squarebill on tapering points near spawning flats in under 10 feet; 3) Late postspawn: deep diver following...
Wheeler: The thermocline sets up deeper (25-30 feet) in clear-water reservoirs and shallower (8-10 feet) in murky waters; rivers generally don't have consistent thermoclines due to flowing water

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal summer bass feed aggressively. Fish split between shallow morning patterns (2-6ft) and offshore structure (12-25ft). Target grass lines, points, and ledges as falling pressure triggers movement.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
